question: Where was Micah from (Mic 1:1)?

The people’s possession is divided up and taken from them
The people’s home are burned with fire

What do their prophets say (Mic 2:6)?
options: Do not tell the people to repent

What do they do to those who pass by without a care (Mic 2:8)?
Who are driven from their pleasant homes (Mic 2:9)?

How will God gather the remnant of Israel (Mic 2:12)?

They will be led by quiet streams

question: What should the leaders of Jacob know (Mic 3:1)?

question: Who will be ashamed (Mic 3:7)?
Why will the seers cover their faces (Mic 3:7)?
options: Because there is no answer from God
Because all their predictions do not come true

question: What does God condemn the rulers of the house of Israel for doing (Mic 3:9)?
options: Not listening to the voice of the Lord

question: What do their leaders judge on the basis of (Mic 3:11)?
What do their priests do for a price (Mic 3:11)?
What do prophets who tell fortunes for money ask (Mic 3:11)?
options: Is not this the word of the Lord
Is not this what will happen to you
Is not the Lord among us
Is not God a forgiving God

question: What do the prophets assume based on the fact that God was among them (Mic 3:11)?
options: No disaster would come upon them
What they said was God’s word

options: Jerusalem will be burned with fire
The gates of Zion will be broken
Zion will be plowed like a field
Blood will flow down from Zion’s heights

question: What will happen because of the false prophets (Mic 3:12)?
options: The temple hill will become overgrown with thickets
Jerusalem will be burned with fire
The gates of Zion will be broken
Blood will flow down from Zion’s heights

question: What will be established in the last days (Mic 4:1)?
options: The mountain of the Lord’s temple

question: What will happen when the mountain of the Lord’s temple is raised above the hills (Mic 4:1)?
options: All the world will bow before it
The Lord will descend with a shout
The glorious cloud will abide there

question: What will many nations say (Mic 4:2)?
options: Let us beat our swords into plowshares
Let us go up to the mountain of the Lord
Let us rest In the presence of the Lord

question: Where will many nations say they want to go (Mic 4:2)?

To gather with the saints of Israel
To enter the gates of the holy city
To the house of the God of Jacob

question: What will go out from Zion (Mic 4:2)?

options: He will lead them into the city

question: In the last days what will the nations do with their swords (Mic 4:3)?
options: Turn them over to the Lord

No one will make them afraid

options: In the light of his glory
In the name of the Lord our God

Weep and mourn like a dove

options: Those Israelites who have gone after other gods
